specification.topic-statement: The event render formatting system transforms stored tool call and tool result records into display-ready strings.

scope.in-scope: Formatting tool call records and tool result records for display, including prefix detection, JSON extraction, fallback to raw content, and null output handling.
scope.boundary: This topic submits a tool call structure (id, name, arguments) and receives a formatted, truncated display string. The format and truncation rules are defined by the tool call formatting system.
This topic submits a tool name and output string and receives a formatted, truncated display string. The format and truncation rules are defined by the tool result formatting system.
This topic creates a format buffer, appends text to it, and retrieves the resulting string. Truncation is applied during append.

field.name: tool_call_id
field.note: required; if absent or not a string, falls back to raw content

field.name: tool_name
field.note: required; if absent or not a string, falls back to raw content

field.name: tool_args
field.note: required; if absent or not a string, falls back to raw content

field.name: name
field.note: required; if absent or not a string, falls back to raw formatter

field.name: output
field.note: if absent or not a string, null is passed to result formatter

behavior.format-tool-call:
A stored tool call content string and its associated data JSON string are submitted for display formatting.
If the content string is non-null and begins with the tool request prefix, the content string is returned as-is without further processing.
If the data JSON string is null, the content string is returned as-is.
The data JSON string is parsed. If parsing fails, the content string is returned as-is.
The parsed object is checked for string fields named "tool_call_id", "tool_name", and "tool_args". If any of these fields is absent or is not a string value, the JSON document is released and the content string is returned as-is.
The three string values are copied into the allocation context. A tool call structure is assembled from the copied strings and submitted to the tool call formatting system. The formatted, truncated result is returned. The JSON document is released before returning.
behavior.format-tool-result:
A stored tool result content string and its associated data JSON string are submitted for display formatting.
If the content string is non-null and begins with the tool response prefix, the content string is returned as-is without further processing.
If the data JSON string is null, the raw tool result formatter is applied to the content string and its output is returned.
The data JSON string is parsed. If parsing fails, the raw tool result formatter is applied to the content string and its output is returned.
The parsed object is checked for a string field named "name". If the field is absent or is not a string value, the JSON document is released and the raw tool result formatter is applied to the content string.
A field named "output" is also checked. If it is present and is a string value, it is used as the output passed to the tool result formatter. If it is absent or not a string, null is passed instead.
When the data JSON parses successfully and contains a valid "name" field, the original content parameter is not used. The display output is derived entirely from the "name" and "output" fields of the data JSON, even if the original content string differs from those values.
The tool name and output (or null) are submitted to the tool result formatting system. The formatted, truncated result is returned. The JSON document is released before returning.
behavior.format-tool-result-raw:
A raw content string is formatted with the tool response prefix applied and the content truncated. This path is taken when no data JSON is available or parseable for a tool result.
The tool response prefix followed by a space is appended to a new format buffer.
If the content string is null, the literal text "(no output)" is appended. Otherwise, the content string is truncated and appended.
The resulting string from the format buffer is returned.
